mission.title: Install and Configure Athenz on Kubernetes
mission.description: Athenz is an open-source platform for X.509 certificate-based service authentication and fine-grained access control in dynamic infrastructures. You would install it to secure service communications and manage access control effectively in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Clone the Athenz repository
steps.description:
Clone the Athenz repository to your local machine to access the deployment manifests.
```bash
git clone https://github.com/AthenZ/athenz.git
cd athenz
```

steps.title: Apply the Kubernetes manifests
steps.description:
Use kubectl to apply the necessary Kubernetes manifests for Athenz deployment. Ensure you are in the correct directory where the manifests are located.
```bash
kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/ --namespace athenz --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the Athenz deployment
steps.description:
Check that all Athenz pods are running successfully in the athenz namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace athenz
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the deployment to set resource limits for the Athenz pods to ensure they have adequate resources.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment athenz --namespace athenz
# Add resource limits under spec.containers.resources
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Athenz
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow Athenz to access the necessary resources.
```bash
kubectl apply -f rbac.yaml --namespace athenz
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Athenz deployment
uninstall.description:
Remove the Athenz resources from your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete -f deploy/kubernetes/ --namespace athenz
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the athenz namespace to remove any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace athenz
```

upgrade.title: Backup current deployment
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of Athenz to a YAML file for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace athenz -o yaml > athenz-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Pull the latest changes
upgrade.description:
Fetch the latest changes from the Athenz repository.
```bash
cd athenz
git pull origin main
```

upgrade.title: Apply the updated manifests
upgrade.description:
Reapply the updated Kubernetes manifests to upgrade Athenz.
```bash
kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/ --namespace athenz
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Athenz pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace athenz
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your Athenz pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace athenz
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods failing with CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are failing with CrashLoopBackOff, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace athenz
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Athenz service is not reachable, verify the service configuration and endpoints.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace athenz
kubectl describe svc <service-name> --namespace athenz
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Athenz will have all pods running in the athenz namespace, and you will be able to access the Athenz services as intended. Resource limits and RBAC configurations will be properly set.
codeSnippets:
kubectl edit deployment athenz --namespace athenz
# Add resource limits under spec.containers.resources

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
